candidate_problems.text: What exactly makes the proposed invariants constitutive of agency rather than merely correlated with it? The conjecture names counterfactual sensitivity, responsiveness to reasons, and error-correcting continuity as legitimacy-preserving invariants, but leaves open why these are the right ones, whether they are jointly sufficient, and how to distinguish genuine agency from sophisticated simulations, manipulated systems, or institutions that mimic these properties.

How can a higher-level 'emergent control structure' be made precise without collapsing into either reductionism or empty relabeling? The conjecture depends on agency having a distinctive causal role within deterministic physics, but it remains unresolved how higher-level reasons, deliberation, and self-correction exert explanatory or causal significance beyond substrate description, and what criteria would mark this as more than pragmatic shorthand.

Where should responsibility boundaries be drawn when agency is understood as a corrigible feedback system vulnerable to bypass, distortion, and design? The conjecture says responsibility remains legitimate when the loop is intact, but it leaves open how to operationalize intactness across coercion, addiction, mental illness, algorithmic manipulation, developmental immaturity, or engineered interventions, and how legal and moral practices should change as causal knowledge improves.
